Yep—good question. I’ll compute this the strict way: “lines in the transitive import closure of the proof entrypoint,” then show what’s left outside `Q2Presentation/*.lean`.

You can’t directly ask Lean “how many lines were *used* by proof search,” but you can get the honest “compiled-under-root” count by using the root import graph.

Use this command (it follows `Q2Presentation.lean`’s transitive imports, then sums only those local source lines):

```bash
cd [local path]
python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess

root = Path('[local path]')
deps = subprocess.check_output(
    ["lake","env","lean","--deps","Q2Presentation.lean"], text=True
)

q2_src = []
for ln in deps.splitlines():
    if '/Q2Presentation/' in ln and ln.endswith('.olean'):
        rel = Path(ln).relative_to(root/'.lake/build/lib/lean').with_suffix('.lean')
        q2_src.append(root/rel)

q2_src = sorted(set(q2_src))
total = sum(sum(1 for _ in p.read_text().splitlines()) for p in q2_src)
nonempty = sum(sum(1 for L in p.read_text().splitlines() if L.strip()) for p in q2_src)

# include the root file itself
root_file = root/'Q2Presentation.lean'
r_total = len(root_file.read_text().splitlines())
r_non = sum(1 for L in root_file.read_text().splitlines() if L.strip())

print("Imported local modules:", len(q2_src))
print("Compiled-local lines (excluding root):", total, "non-empty:", nonempty)
print("Plus root:", r_total, "non-empty:", r_non)
print("Total compiled lines (incl root):", total + r_total)
print("Total compiled non-empty lines (incl root):", nonempty + r_non)
PY
```
